extraction.one_line_summary: RODRÍGUEZ Óscar bridged to MAJKA and TEUNS in final 1.5km of 14% finish, dropped them with superior steep-gradient power; QUINTANA took 6 seconds from YATES in GC group sprint.
tactical_patterns: Movistar controlled entire stage despite not holding leader's jersey, protecting dual-podium ambitions of QUINTANA and VALVERDE simultaneously.
32-rider breakaway given excessive leeway, suggesting GC teams banking energy reserves across three consecutive summit finishes rather than controlling early.
MAJKA attacked breakaway 1.5km out but cracked when RODRÍGUEZ bridged, revealing category-one riders underestimate specialists on 14% pitches.
QUINTANA-YATES rode shoulder-to-shoulder final 3km then QUINTANA accelerated only final 50m, gaming sprint rather than distancing on gradient.

rider_signals.RODRÍGUEZ Óscar: positive: bridged 1.5km gap on 14% gradient then immediately dropped MAJKA and TEUNS, exceptional steep-climb acceleration capacity
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: won previous edition this climb, took 6 seconds from YATES with late acceleration, Movistar building dual-podium strategy around him
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: matched QUINTANA until final 50m but conceded sprint, possibly conserving for Sunday's Lagos de Covadonga queen stage
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafał: negative: attacked breakaway early but cracked when RODRÍGUEZ bridged, insufficient power-to-weight for 14% finishes despite being Bora GC option
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: neutral: reached final km with MAJKA but dropped by RODRÍGUEZ's bridge, competitive but lacking decisive punch
rider_signals.KING Ben: positive: second overall in KOM competition, consistently infiltrating breakaways to accumulate mountain points across multiple stages
rider_signals.DE GENDT Thomas: neutral: aggressively hunting KOM points in breakaway but unable to contest steep finish
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: top-five GC with QUINTANA, Movistar protecting dual-podium options rather than committing to single leader
